# Functional release proof

Release SHA: `04196921264d9897e8e547a8e0a2cef1ad43f8fb`  
Preview: <https://ryanholtschneider2.github.io/soloco-product-build-v2-dogfood/>  
Result: PASS

The anonymous customer journey was driven in Chromium against the deployed GitHub Pages product. The initial action remained disabled until the seeded service was selected. Invalid email produced associated inline guidance and preserved entered data. A keyboard submission created one cryptographically shaped `PB-` reference, moved focus to the confirmation region, and stored one normalized record under `proofbook.booking.v1`.

Reload restored the same reference with “Restored from this browser.” Clearing the record returned focus to the service selector and allowed a second booking. At 390x844, a controlled 750 ms frame delay exposed `aria-busy="true"`, the disabled action, and “Saving proof…”; a duplicate activation still produced one record and one reference.

Controlled release-proof injections exercised real error handling at the deployed SHA:

- storage write failure retained the form data, focused the assertive status, stored nothing, and displayed no confirmation;
- an empty catalog rendered the unavailable state with no dead action;
- malicious malformed local storage was discarded, created no injected image or script, and returned the customer to the form.

The exact release checkout also passed `make format lint test`: 8 Python tests and 6 Node tests.
